name: Exxon Mobil Corp. v. Corporación Cimex, S.A. (Cuba)
short_description: A Helms-Burton Act case testing the relationship between Title III and foreign sovereign immunity defenses.
legal_question: Whether Title III of the LIBERTAD (Helms-Burton) Act abrogates foreign sovereign immunity for suits against Cuban instrumentalities, or whether a plaintiff must also satisfy an exception under the Foreign Sovereign Immunities Act.
petitioner_argument_summary: Petitioner argues Congress created a direct cause of action under Helms-Burton that permits Title III suits against Cuban instrumentalities and that FSIA immunity should not bar those claims.
respondent_argument_summary: Respondent argues FSIA provides the default rule of immunity for foreign states and their instrumentalities and that Helms-Burton does not displace FSIA absent a clearly applicable exception.
